clinical_history: 34 year old woman presents with diastolic CHF syndrome and a hypertrophic cardiomyopathy variant. She was taking amiodarone for recurrent atrial tachyarrhythmias. The ECG shows all the following EXCEPT?
diagnosis: The ECG shows sinus bradycardia at 50 bpm. The QTc is prolonged consistent with the amiodarone. The P waves are strikingly prominent, tall and wide, consistent with biatrial abnormality confirmed by echocardiogram, which also showed an idiopathic hypertrophic type cardiomyopathy. A left ventricular IVCD is present with slow R wave progression. No coronary disease was present.  The ECG does not show epsilon waves (rapid ST deflections in leads V1-V2 due to right ventricular cardiompathy and associated local fragmented electrograms.)
